raw_consumer_statement:
XX/XX/XXXX : I opened dispute XXXX with Citi against XXXX for $ XXXX I had not received an item I ordered and was not getting a response from the merchant. 

XX/XX/XXXX : Received the merchandise from XXXX XX/XX/XXXX : I withdrew my dispute as I received the item and opened the dispute originally for not receiving it and believed that the matter was resolved XX/XX/XXXX - XX/XX/XXXX : Noticed that the item received was not in working order/as described and reached out to the merchant to request a refund and submitted evidence showing the issue which the merchant rejected. 

XX/XX/XXXX : Called Citi to open a new dispute as the item I received was not as described. Was told that Citi would reopen my dispute and change the dispute type XX/XX/XXXX : Called Citi as the dispute did not appear to be reopened and attempted to open a new dispute. Representative that I spoke with stated that they would reopen the original case and would change the type of dispute. I was instructed to submit supporting documents to XXXX and to include the dispute number XX/XX/XXXX : I submitted all the evidence that I had showing the merchandise I received was not in working order and the response I received from the merchant denying my request for refund. 

XX/XX/XXXX : I received a letter from Citi stating that I was not allowed to reopen my dispute and would have to work it out with the merchant. 

The reason why I attempted to open a new dispute ( or reopen the original dispute and change the dispute reason ) because the merchant was refusing to work with me and resolve the issue. I do not see anywhere in my credit card agreement where I was not able to open multiple disputes on a transaction if they are for different reasons. In the agreement it states that I am not responsible for fraudulent transactions ( which I consider this to be since the merchant did not deliver the item as described/in working condition ). I believe that Citi is in violation of their own agreement for refusing to open a new dispute for a different reason on a charge just because I opened a previous dispute. Both disputes are for valid reasons
